review_text: Service is very fast, food is good. Never been there for a game day, but they have many TV's. Monday nights they seem to be very busy, maybe a drink special?

I had been going here for years, and the service has always been poor, something my friends and I overlook because we enjoy the food so much. Our most recent experience left us all speechless. We were overcharged, and the manager and server were the most rude service people I've ever encountered; not just in a restaurant, but anywhere. From the minute we walked in, we were treated in a hostile way by the server, who couldn't understand anything we were saying. As a result of this, we received things that we didn't order, and didn't receive things we actually ordered. When I approached the manager, he said that "there are other places I can go". He had no interest in accommodating us, and frankly, I feel like he was making up policies on the spot. Worst restaurant experience of my life. Never go to a Los Tres if you can help it.
Very good food and service. Try the Nachos Fajita Steak. It is the best.

I like the food here, but I have to share this review on principal...
The big sign out front promotes 3.99 Margaritas.  So I go in on my lunch, order a $6.50 lunch combo and a couple margaritas.  The bill comes to $23!!  I look, and they were charging me for the $7.99 margaritas!  This wouldn't be so bad if I actually drank a margarita that was worth $7.99, but these were snifter sized glasses full of ice.   I was offered two sixes, the Large, and the next size down. I figured that was the appropriately priced dink.
The margarita was OK to the taste, but was an incredible ripoff for the price.
If you are going in for food and drink, you might want to confirm the prices, if money matters to you.
Best Mexican food in the area. Very affordable, and always delicious. Half of appetizers and beer every night.
These guys double charged my credit card and acted like it was my fault.  They hung up on me twice.  Don't eat at any Los Tres Amigos they do not care about their customers.
Awesome service. Grest food.
Best Tacos Ever

Well what can I say other than they need to communicate to their employees and their customers. I placed an order (130 dollars) for pickup on July 4th at 6:30 PM before my party. I was told on June 27, and July 1 that they WOULD be open on July 4th with normal hours (I called on July 1 just to be safe, because when I placed the order, no one seemed to know anything) and that I could pick it up, as I had payed in advance. Well, luckily I got there At 5 30, one hour before, to find they were CLOSED! I was absolutely livid. I have never had something like this happen. I gave them my phone number, name, and address, and I was not told that they would be closed even after confirming several times. This is honestly ridiculous and could've ruined my party. Luckily, Asia's Finest just down the road came in clutch and saved it. Never eating here again. I am expecting a full refund for food I NEVER RECEIVED! Make this right, management.

We came in very hungry. We were given a menu and our drink orders were promptly taken. My first clue that this may not be a good experience was when I overheard the table of 5 across from us say they had been sitting for 10 minutes and their drinks had not yet arrived.
A few minutes after that our waiter who had taken our drink order brought another tables food to us...we had not been approached to order food yet. He should have known that!!
We waited for over 10 min (from the time I started timing...not right away after order was taken) for drinks we never got. Which sucks on a lot of different levels; I had tried a hot sauce that was too hot, so my wine would have been great. We didn't even have waters. As we were leaving they sat a big table. Good luck to them. My husband said there wasn't anyone behind the bar when he went looking for our drinks. They really weren't that busy when we first walked in. No excuse for that kind of service. There were at least 4 workers and as far as I could tell 4 table seated. I'd pass this place up if I were you. Unless you just want free chips and salsa before walking out without dinner or drinks.

review_text: I've gotten takeout from here three times over the past couple of months. When I called to order, the girl who took my order was very coherent and when I asked her to read my order back to me she had everything correct. But, there seems to be a miscommunication between the person who takes your oder and the cook who makes the food. I ordered burritos, both the exact same with no cheese sauce on the outside but both of them came with cheese sauce on them. Then I ordered two steak tacos which were at least $4-5 a piece and they were so small they should have been called steak roll ups, i'm not joking they were one bite. I don't understand why its so hard to make someones food exactly the way they tell you to. Theres really no excuse for the miscommunication between staff and the size of the tacos were really a smack in the face. I also got food poisoning the next morning, so thats great.

review_text:
10/31/22 update: I just ordered a taco salad, 2 small chips, 1 small salsa and 2 sides of rice.  I ordered thru Doordash. It came on time. Taco salad had a light layer of ground beef laying at the bottom of the "tortilla" bowl. The meat was absolutely cold. NOT even slightly warm. I scooped the mixture from the container onto a plate to heat it up and it all fell to pieces. It was loaded with nothing but salad and sour cream. Oh, I almost forgot, it came with tomato. Yes, 1 round slice of tomato. They didn't even bother to chop it. Just threw it right on in there. 🤦🏽‍♀️ wanna know how much this slop cost? $30 total, with tip.  NEVER AGAIN!  How do you even get your $$ back??

I love their house margaritas 🤘  Tonight, I had the shrimp fajita dinner. It came with a side of warm flour tortillas, Pico de gallo, sour cream and guacamole, rice & beans. My son had the steak fajita dinner.  I had the house margarita.  The food was great!  Everything was $34 and change.   This place can be a hit & miss spot. Sometimes the food and service is fantastic or not. But either way, you can't go wrong with the  margaritas. 😉
owner_response: Hello Alice, thank you for visiting us. Have a wonderful day.

review_text: I love this place! They have great food & drinks. The reason I gave them 3 stars is that, like many "chain" Mexican restaurants, they're too expensive & I always leave hungry! They charge around $15+ per plate, but most of their meals aren't any bigger than an appetizer & that doesn't include a drink. So for a meal & a drink, per person, roughly $20-25 & afterwards, you'll have to go to McD's or BK to get full! If you're in a big rush...these guys are FAST! I've been in & out, eaten a full meal, in 20 minutes! Good food, quickly, but overpriced for the amount of food! If they'd give generous portions, they'd easily be 4+ stars, but I leave hungry every single time...I'm 5'10" & 150#, so not a huge guy!
owner_response: Hello Mike, we are so sorry to read that you are not getting completely satisfied with our food. We will consider your comment about the prices for future modifications. Thank you for taking the time to tell us your experience.

review_text: This location is easy enough to see from the road and has a decent parking lot with plenty of parking for the low number of diners that are there at any given time. There are some rough patches and small pot holes but nothing too bad. The patio was well kept and landscaping was being worked on. It looked like the landscaping was much needed and was going well to improve the appearance of the exterior. The food is pretty good and the new menu is nice with lots of different options. The fresh tortilla chips are very good but the salsa is more of a mild tomato-ey liquid than a fresh chunky salsa with bite. There is a festive atmosphere with bright colors, murals and several TVs around the bar area. Tables, booths, and high tops or bar seating are available. I did not use the restroom but I have no reason to believe that it would be unacceptable. The restaurant was fairly clean and well kept but not as much as it could be. The cleanliness was certainly acceptable, though. The service was good. We were seated quickly and our waitress was fairly attentive. The ice tea was weak but not undrinkable. Overall a good experience and I would return and would recommend to this restaurant to friends.

review_text:
We came last night for a birthday celebration (with about 40 people). We didn’t all come at the same time, but the restaurant was informed to expect around 30 people (yes, we ended up having 10 extra people there). Instead of accommodating the situation, the manager got really upset with us and started yelling and slamming things on the table (because there was too many paying customers there). After we ALL were seated, there were still MANY EMPTY TABLES in the restaurant, so I don’t understand why she was so rude about it.
I have never heard of a restaurant that didn’t want to have people there..
Also, we had ONE server for all of us... so no refills on drinks, or chips. It even took forever for us to have silverware! The server did the best that she could, but I have to give 1 star for the unprofessionalism of the management.
This restaurant lost the business of many of us tonight. That was my first time yesterday and I will never be back again.
If you have a celebration, I strongly advice you to go anywhere else!

review_text:
May 14, 2019
Tonight at 7, my wife and daughter took me out to dinner at the Cedar St location, South Lansing. My wife drove, and they were taking me out for Mexican food. I was not driving. I was not paying the bill. The young waitress came to the table to tale our drink order. My daughter wanted a coke, my wife wanted nothing and I wanted a Cabernet. She asked to see my driver license. I explained that I had not brought my wallet with me. She said she would ask the manager, and came back with the statement that I could not have wine without a picture ID. I asked to speak with the manager who insisted that they could not legally serve anyone without an actual picture ID showing their birth date.

I am 70 years old. I had my phone with digitized copies of my AARP card and my Medicaid card. I was told that none of that mattered. I have never been treated so poorly in a restaurant, and I have not been asked for picture ID when ordering alcohol since I was 25.

Has anyone else over 65 been carded here or anywhere else? ...and refused service because they were not driving, thus having no need to carry their wallet?

I have seldom felt so embarrassed, ill used, angry or disrespected.

The waitress didn't make any effort to move us nor did  managment. review_text: The server was terrible! She was obviously not paying attention during ordering, rushing, not writing much, and never repeated the order to us to clarify, like most servers do. She brought out the appitizer along with the meal (mushroom quesadilla-a disaster in itself since it was more like a wet mushroom taco-how could one share that?) never brought my husband a water, nor refilled my or my son's beverage, brought a completely wrong meal for my husband (something he would never order.) When we shook our heads in shock at the wrong meal, she told us "Well that's what i wrote down! I'll show you what I wrote down for your order. You want me to put in for something else?" Getting the idea she would tamper with anything sent back, we shook our heads. On top of her terrible service, my meal (spinach chicken) was Far too salty, my son's chicken+ rice had no flavor whatsoever, and my hubs meal went untouched. So, we didn't feel like finishing our meal, let alone paying a tip for our poor service. Not to mention the ruined evening as a family which we get So infrequently.

review_text: I've always found the food here to be somewhat bland.  Maybe that's just my tastes.  But lacking any real flavor in most of the dishes I've had, even though they seem pretty fresh.  Part of why I'm not giving a higher rating is a very simple reason.  It seems dirty to me.  Maybe not the entire restaurant, necessarily, but one thing I noticed very easily is, to me, a red flag.  Look at the inside of the door that leads out of the restaurant.  It's stained with dirt and grease.  Most people don't think to look twice at the door as they are leaving - but in a restaurant where everything is deep cleaned you won't find a dirt mark on the inside of the door leading out.  It's very easy to clean.  Even if its stained, it can be scrubbed.  Even if the scrubbing removes some of the paint to get the dirt off, painting a door isn't very difficult either.  I used to think that Finley's was a good restaurant long ago, and at some point, the door leading out started developing that build up of dirt and grease, and shortly thereafter it closed.  The door may be being sanitized now, but worn in dirt and grease isn't something I want to associate with eating at any restaurant.

review_text:
On Tuesday, March 29, 2022 at 6:00 PM I placed an order for a combo platter of hors d’oeuvres and a couple tacos. The 20% tip, the fees and the food came out to just under $50. Two hours later I contacted the restaurant and delivery.com by phone to find out what the issue was.

The guy that answered the phone at los Tres Amigos said , “It’s the [Expletive] delivery guy, he never processed the order, showed up and then left! We don’t have an order from you.” And they were unable to take a direct delivery order from me as well. I understand it’s about the system and how the system works. But there Hass to be a better way. Either make the service work or don’t offer it Is what I would suggest.

When I called delivery.com they called the restaurant the restaurant said that they had been having issues receiving orders, (plural.) I get it that things happen but when your order just gets canceled after waiting two hours and it was never prepared to begin with, that shows me that this is a flawed system altogether.

It certainly sounds like this is an issue that exists not only with Los Tres Amigos on Cedar Street in Lansing but also with delivery.com. If it were my business and I felt that a third-party was making my business look bad and creating customer service issues I would certainly sever my relationship with them. In this case it sounds like it was an issue on both sides.

When I’ve eaten there in the past the food has been great . This particular order has been a disaster (and especially frustrating for a $49 plate of tacos.) For that much I would think that they could work together and figure it out. I will also be placing a review for delivery.com in a similar fashion.

I suggest you just not offer the takeout service if this is a routine problem. As good as the food is at this restaurant I don’t think this relationship with delivery.com is a positive one. I know I am severing that business relationship myself.
owner_response: Hi Mark, we suggest to use the site to pick from our website we have no relation with delivery.com you can also dm us over facebook so we can better help you.

review_text:
Authentic Mexican Experience! 🇲🇽🌮🌯

Rating: ⭐⭐⭐⭐

Los Tres Amigos offers an authentic Mexican dining experience right here in Holt. Here's what stood out during my visits:

1. Delicious Food: My absolute favorite Mexican restaurant in the 517! 😍 💓 💛 💗 💖 ❤️ 😍 💓 💛  ♥️ ❤️ 👌🏼 ✨️ 🎶 💖 I haven't had a dish yet that I didn't like . You will not be disappointed.

2. Friendly Service: The staff is attentive and welcoming. Even if you're a first-time visitor, they'll make you feel right at home.

3. Fast Service: Food arrives promptly, and the overall dining experience is efficient.

While some reviewers have mentioned minor issues, like inconsistent food quality, my personal experiences have been positive. I recommend dining in for the best experience. 🌮🥑

If you're craving Mexican cuisine, give Los Tres Amigos a visit!
owner_response: Thank you for your recommendation

review_text:
Today..

Los tres amigos. Holt

Sat at the bar, took my jacket off, waiting for someone to wait on me. A few minutes later a gentleman asked if I had a to go order. I said no I would like to eat here.  (reason why I’m sitting at the bar with my jacket off.). He gave me a menu. I had to ask for a drink. I had to ask for chips and salsa. And I had to ask for silverware after I was given my food. I still had about half a drink and some chips and salsa and was asked by same person if I wanted to pay my tab.  I told him, when I’m done.   I didn’t have a card or cash out to give him the idea I was ready to pay. Never felt very welcome. And Felt rushed.  They’re not very busy so why try to rush me out the door?
I like to tip 25% or more especially if my bill isn’t very much like today then I may match the bill, but the tip is for the quality of service you receive which I feel wasn’t very good this time. Could have gotten a much bigger tip.
Alexander. You may want to make your customers feel welcomed and not rushed.

2 years ago….  I’ve always loved Los Tres Amigos!!!  Love their chips and salsa as well as their food. Great margaritas too!!!
owner_response: Thank you for taking the time to provide us with your feedback, Lanie. We truly do value all perspectives in order to enhance our service. It's disheartening to hear that your dining experience was not up to the standards we hold for ourselves. We fully realize the frustration that might come from feeling rushed, especially in a hospitable setting like ours. Encouraging our guests to feel relaxed and cared for is truly at the center of our mission. We apologize for the lack of attentiveness you experienced during your visit and are certainly grateful for your insights.

review_text:
I was craving birria tacos tonight, so I headed straight to the restaurant's website to place my order. I was going to be picking the order up, so I figured it would be easier to order directly through the restaurant rather than through a delivery app. Excitedly, I ordered three servings of their birria tacos, along with sides of rice and beans for each order. However, upon arriving home and delving into our meal, we discovered that the rice and beans were missing. Disappointed but hopeful for a quick resolution, I called the restaurant, expecting a simple refund for the missing sides. We had already eaten, so obviously we no longer needed the sides. I assumed it would be no problem for them to just issue me a refund – even if I had to come pick the refund up.

Unfortunately, the situation quickly escalated into a frustrating ordeal. Despite them acknowledging their mistake, they refused to issue a refund. Instead, the guy on the phone argued with me about it, offered to only give me ONE order of rice and beans (out of the THREE that I had ordered… which is WILD), and then subsequently hung up on me! Extremely unprofessional. I was not yelling or rude to him; he simply did not want to handle the situation, and just hung up!

My following in-person visit didn’t make things any better. While the woman I spoke with in person was nicer than the guy over the phone, there was still a lack of willingness to rectify THEIR error. She stated that my order had gone through “Bento Box'' and did not come directly through them, so that was the reason they could not refund me. I still fail to see how that’s MY problem when I ordered directly from the restaurant’s website. Whatever partnership they may have with Bento Box has zero to do with me. They are obviously still going to receive my money one way or another, it’s not like they fed me out of the kindness of their hearts. So I still don’t understand why they would refuse to issue a refund, regardless of which platform my order went through. At this point, I think it would’ve actually been easier to have ordered through a delivery app. At least I could have requested a refund.

On top of that, despite them having my order in black-and-white right in front of them, it still took ten minutes of explanation for them to finally realize that I actually ordered (and PAID for) THREE sides instead of just ONE. Apparently, since all of the orders were for the exact same thing, they somehow missed the fact that the order said “x3”, which means I ordered THREE of the same thing. Either the employees need to be trained on this, or their website checkout needs to be redesigned. It’s ridiculous that I had to PROVE what I ordered and paid for. Two different employees were ADAMANT that I had only made one order, until they did the math themselves and realized that I had INDEED paid for 3!

Either way, it didn’t matter because I still did NOT receive my refund. It's disheartening when a simple request for what's rightfully mine turns into something like this. They made a mistake and forgot our sides – I get that. It happens. But the fact that they seem to have no way whatsoever to account for the possibility of this happening is crazy to me. There was zero accountability. It’s like they just said, “Oh well!!” and threw their hands in the air. No attempt whatsoever to rectify the situation. The guy who hung up in my face also left a terrible impression of this restaurant. As a business, that is NOT how you handle a dispute. As a person who values integrity with a huge emphasis on customer service, I expected more from a business where I’m spending my hard-earned money. If it had been my own business, especially knowing that it was MY mistake, I would have done what I could to satisfy the customer and worried about the logistics later. Instead, they put logistics ahead of customer service, and as a result, have lost a customer.
owner_response:
Dear Kyli,

We are very sorry to hear about your experience with us. 
We will be speaking with management to prevent this from happening in the future. 
We hope you can give us another chance!

Thank you and have a great morning
Thank you for your review

review_text: Food tasted good, prices were good and service was fast and friendly. But food didn't agree with me. Indigestion pretty soon after and throwing up the next day. You may have a different constitution than mine that handles what felt like a lot of  grease better than mine, so I wouldn't avoid this restaurant due to my lackluster review. We all react differently to different types of food. Could be I was just coming down with something and it has nothing to do with the food too in fairness, but this restaurant just isn't my top choice for Mexican food. I prefer Acapulco in East Lansing for similar foods that feel less heavy/ greasy to me and a menu that has more options on the lighter side. My table mate enjoyed her dish and was not ill after, so my experience may have been more about my body than the food.

review_text:
I wasnt going to rate but it popped up asking me to so...
We've had good service in the past, but it had been many months since dining at this location. We usually dine at the Mason location as its closer. My daughter picked the Holt Los Tres Amigos (because she likes the building and its a change from the normal) to celebrate her 7th birthday with family. We called ahead to see about making a reservation and were told they don't take reservations, but 9 people wouldn't be an issue. We arrived at 430 and they thought we were a reservation they had for 11 people at 6pm I let them know we were not and hadn't made a reservation as we were told they didn't take them when we had called a few hours ago.  We were seated and the young women acted like we were a burden right from the begining. The other waiter and the blonde waitress acted very differently to their tables, happy and checked in on them, so it didnt seem like the whole atmosphere was negative. Our waitress, she just seemed put off by us the whole time and didn't really check back. I gave my daughter my drink since our waitress didn't come check on things. The blonde waitress seemed to notice because she asked if we needed anything and we did get refills and napkins.  It was awkward and I will just go to mason from now on. Hopefully the young women was having an off day, but she definitely made it uncomfortable. We just tried to eat and get out. There was talk of leaving a lower tip and we decided to still tip well due to the holidays and hope it could raise the grumpy lady's spirits so she might act nicer to her next table.
If we go back I will speak up and just leave if need be, we just didn't want to further dampen the spirits for the birthday girl.

I will add we didn't bring any outside food like cupcakes, balloons or anything to be in the way etc. My daughter got birthday cards and one small gift.  I made sure we ordered before letting her open the cards so we wouldn't hold up the restaurant.
owner_response: Thank you for sharing your experience. We appreciate your support, especially during your daughter’s special celebration. It’s unfortunate to hear about the service issues you encountered during your visit. We aim to provide a welcoming atmosphere for all our guests. Your feedback will help us address these matters and improve moving forward. We hope to create a better experience for you in the future.

review_text: They had a fantastic late night deal for half price drinks and appetizers after 8 pm. This deal was not only advertised on their current website, but also on their Facebook page and a local ad catalog we received not more than a couple weeks prior. Seeing the advertisement was what brought us to the Saginaw location Friday night and we enjoyed it so much we planned to go to the Holt location the next night while we were nearby. I started the order by explaining we wanted to share a few appetizers between the three of us since they’re half off after 8. He said he would have to go check with his manager who came back to the table and explained that only certain locations were still offering it and they weren’t because “it wasn’t working out for them.” It’s very frustrating considering all of their advertisements are still active and they weren’t willing to make any sort of accommodation considering that was the main reason we went in that night. Hopefully they will change it on their website or at least share a post that they don’t offer it anymore so no one else has to be disappointed by it.

review_text: I usually enjoy my time here my favorite location to be exact. The service took so long i ended up packing my dinner to go as soon as she sat it at the table. When i got home and began eating the beans and rice I hit a wired hard squeezed against my teeth pice of something, thank God i was able to sift it out of the food in my mouth because THERE WAS GLASS IN MY BEANS!!!! Not one but two small broken prices. Idk who’s mistake it was idc who’s mistake it was if a glass of some kind brakes by an open container that whole container should have been tossed and honestly still thinking about calling the health department because what else could you accidentally do?! I literally could have died from internal bleeding because my use to be favorite place is trying to cut corners and save money. Next time just toss the damn beans.

review_text: I haven’t ate at Los Tres in years since another Mexican restaurant opened closer to where I live. I was visiting a friend in Holt so we decided to try this location bc Mexican food sounded good so we decided to try it because I knew I did really like it back in the day. The service & the atmosphere was great. The food was not. I ordered the sample platter with Mexican rice on the side. I ordered the chimichangas with chicken the other things with beef. The beef had the weirdest taste to it, like it could have been going bad. The chimichangas & the cheese quesadilla were the only things that were edible. The rice was so bland & flavorless. I was really disappointed & won’t be going back.

review_text: Good food, but beware of pouty servers. We've been coming here for years and the food has always been good. We each had the carne asada tacos (3 taco dinner) this weekend. They were tasty as usual, but the amount of meat per taco varied considerably. Some were stuffed full, while others were almost empty. Odd. Our server was fast and friendly, but basically did the minimum (took order, brought food and drinks out, offered one drink refill). When it came time to pay the bill, I left a decent amount. I leave flat amounts, since tipping based on percentage of the bill makes no sense. I don't recall the exact "recommended" percentages, but I believe they started at 25% and went up from there, which is ridiculous. Well, apparently the server was hoping for a bigger tip. In an instance, his entire attitude changed. He became super quiet, no longer smiled, and basically just walked away. We hung around for a bit, then left the restaurant. We passed him on the way out, and he wouldn't even look at us. I was shocked to see such behavior.
owner_response: Thank you for your feedback, Robert. We're glad to hear that you enjoyed our carne asada tacos. Apologies if our service did not live up to your expectations. Your remarks regarding the variation in portion sizes and the server's behavior were noted. We're always looking for ways to improve the dining experience for our guests. Your suggestions provide valuable insight into where we need to focus our attention. Please, get in touch to further discuss your experience. Thanks again.

review_text: I have never dined in at this location so I can’t speak from that point of view, but I have ordered takeout from them four times now for my family of five and the last three times the takeout order has been missing an entree and very little has been offered to compensate me for this. review_text: The service was terrible honestly. I took my girlfriend and daughter here for lunch  today, Saturday may 12th, and needless to say they didnt care about us at all. I mean from getting my order wrong, failing to refill our salsa and chips, to us having to remind MULTIPLE waiters for straws (which they carry in their aprons), 2 lemons and a booster seat for my daughter. I had to walk to the bar to remind them. They even went as far as to bring me my beer and not my daughter or girlfriends drinks, in which we had to remind them another 3 times for those as well. We were labeled being black and because of that, the level of service was poor, compared to if I were Hispanic or white. **HUGE DISCLAIMER** They treat you differently here if you aren't either of the two and it shows. This in turn speaks VOLUMES on the management as well to employ people of this caliber that are so worried about a tip they fail to do their job to recieve one, because of the fact you aren't Hispanic or white. If i weren't a different person I would have left on the bill. I WILL NEVER BRING MY FAMILY BACK TO THE HOLT LOS TRES AMIGOS EVER AGAIN!!
owner_response: We appreciate your feedback, King. We are deeply sorry to hear about your unpleasant experience. We aim to offer an inclusive and welcoming environment for all our guests, irrespective of their background. It appears we have failed in your case, and for that, we sincerely apologize. It is our priority to make every guest feel valued and well-served. We'd like to discuss your concerns further. We're eager to make this right and hope to serve you better in the future.

review_text: I’ve been to each location so many times :/ but Thursday was so disappointing. Food horrible and service not much better. My waiter was nice but he put shrimp on my food, they just removed the shrimp and brought it Rt back out. No napkins, soup horrible, steak and chicken burnt tasting from sitting on the skillet, no onions and tomatoes for my rice like requested. Entire order sucked. Don’t waste your time reaching out. review_text: The atmosphere of the place is wonderful, but that's where it ends. I had 3 tacos. Fish, chicken and beef. The beef was edible but greasy, the fish had to have been fried either much earlier in the day, or the night before as it was dry, rubbery and discolored. Not to mention void of ANY flavor whatsoever! The chicken was also dry and bland. Devoid of seasoning. NOT EVEN SALT AND PEPPER! The refried pinto beans were from a can as they had a metallic taste to them similar to sucking on a penny. The Mexican rice was partially hard. Not as in undercooked but as being leftover from a previous day  Not a single person in our 12 person enjoyed what they ordered. Unlike them, I brought these issues to light with management. I will NEVER eat there again! The quality of the food ABSOLUTELY sucks! AND was overpriced to boot! The waiter we had was nice, but only stopped by the table once after serving us that garbage. I STRONGLY recommend that if you're looking for GOOD, authentic Mexican cuisine that you avoid Tres Amigos AT ALL COST! As for the 12 of us, NONE of us will EVER eat there again.
owner_response: We appreciate your honesty, Earl. We're sorry to hear about your disappointing experience. Your feedback on our tacos, refried pinto beans, and Mexican rice is taken seriously as it helps us improve. High quality food preparation and service is our goal; we're sorry we missed the mark during your visit. Your comments have been noted for review and action. We hope for an opportunity to revive your impression of our restaurant. Please contact us at promotions@L3amigos.com to discuss this further.

review_text:
I met my parents for dinner here today, and was really shocked to hear that, while waiting for a table, my 70-something year old dad sat on the metal bench outside of the restaurant and the leg collapsed! The legs were rusted and instable- my dad is not overweight by any means. He fell backward and hit the back of his head against the stone wall of the restaurant, and scraped up his back significantly as well. According to my parents, the staff did not seem concerned that this had happened. The actual meal was okay and our server was great, but this really colored our experience and I know my Dad’s back and head still hurt. When we left the restaurant, the bench was still broken, the other next to it just as rusted and (in my opinion) unsafe.

I would have appreciated an obvious attempt to prevent this from happening again (a sign on the benches, caution cone, anything) and a sincere apology for the restaurant’s failure to remove or notice the instability of the benches by their front door. I am just glad my dad wasn’t seriously injured.
owner_response: We're deeply sorry about the unfortunate incident involving your father, Saga. Your feedback is incredibly important to us and we want to make sure what you described doesn't happen to anyone else. We'll ensure our outdoor seating is properly inspected and maintained. We also regret that our response at the time did not meet your expectations. This is an area we will certainly improve on. We appreciate your feedback to help us grow better. Please reach out to us via email. We would like to discuss this further and see what we can do to rectify this situation.

review_text:
Hello I wanted to reach out about a situation that I had with me and my daughter when I dined in for dinner. I was treated poorly and she was as well and the manager made very racial remarks and slurs towards us and was very unprofessional and rude. It was uncalled for and not okay to be treated that way and I will not be back to eat there or recommend this restaurant to any friends or family members. The manager that was working ant the booth in the front of the restaurant that came to my table asked me for my drivers license because he didn’t think I was a mother because I wanted a free dessert for Mother’s Day. He was making comments about me not being my daughter’s Mother. For some reason he didn’t believe that I was her Mother. It wasn’t that serious over getting a free dessert. They had a special going on for Mother’s day where you can get a free dessert. I am white and she is black and white. I called and made a complaint about it and the same guy that I had the issue with answered the phone and I told him the situation and what happened. He said that would never happen here because they’re Mexican people that work there at the restaurant. I told him that it did happen and it can happen to anyone at anytime regardless of race. I told him that was very disrespectful and it shouldn’t have happened in the first place. I don’t understand why I would be asked for my drivers license if I’m not buying alcohol in the first place. That has nothing to do with me getting a free dessert. An id isn’t going to prove anything about me being a Mother. I am very frustrated this happened and that the restaurant treats other people differently because of someone’s race. That is never okay and Is uncalled for and out of line. This isn’t the first time I have had an incident with this manager. He has been very rude and disrespectful to me on several occasions also when getting to go orders. I have had order messed up and I was blamed for their mistakes. I think he should be disciplined and fired for his poor behavior and customer service as well as being prejudice and making racial slurs and remarks. He is a tall guy that is Mexican and I don’t recall his name but he is very serious all the time and not friendly
at all.  I should have not had to deal with this situation or my daughter what so ever. I will never come back here ever again.
owner_response: We truly apologize, Gabriella, for the horrible experience you and your daughter endured while dining with us. We take your accusations seriously and absolutely do not tolerate such behavior. Please know we're addressing this matter seriously to ensure no customer experiences such a situation in the future. Your invaluable feedback has given us an opportunity to reflect and improve. We regret the loss of your patronage.
